column33: Comments

Green and Yellow cards for Hand 1, Hand 2 mostly HQ cleanup and some kill as well. Not a very fun setup, very luck dependent. I only drew 2 Master Strikes, my results would have been much worse if 1 or 2 other Strikes would have shown. Best things happening was rescuing Detective Wolverine and preventing some escape! Side note: Karnak is so good with Drax, will have to try this again with another setup.

Stressful, but managable with Black Swan. Star Lord was honestly just there to be escape fodder or to sit in HQ under sewers to protect me from abomination enemies. After choosing Swan, I saw all 4 heros so far were on different teams. So I invited Captain Britain :). After deducting that the last tactic was rescue 6 bystanders if fought in the city that the VP farming was worth it. Felt comfortable doing so when I could rearrange the top 3 villain cards most turns. Not 5, never found black swans rare :(
Too many bystanders inhibited the melking
Rocket Raccoon saved the day! Each side picked up 1 of his instinct artifacts very early, and from then on, they just accrued shards and prevented the twists from taking rescued bystanders away!!

First game did not go as planned. Still won but 3 thicc Marauders ran through the city before we could stop them. Second game popped off as I had hoped the first game would have by me getting Jeans cards early and then her rare! Made pretty short work of the Kree Management after that. Mist also recruited X's Rare and used it on a henchmen. That was fun too.

column33: Was able to get instinct in both hands so the scheme was never a problem. I got some KO power but not as much as I would've loved to have so it took a few extra turns to get this finished.

So a bad villain deck shuffle is a bagelshuffle, what's a bad hero deck shuffle called? Both games I barely saw any of the numerous KO cards. Literally 1 copy of Boom Goes the Dynamite came up, and not until the second to last turn of game 1.

Thanks to all that have participated in this tournament and those putting it on.
I feel my turn count was too high to move on.
Nightcrawler was great at teleporting until needed and Gamora with Groot held on to shards until needed. Old man Logan and Punisher helped ko a ton.

Had good synergy going, with Iceman stuff in one side, and Jean Grey doing bystander synergy with F4 and White Tiger in other. Iceman gave epic last turn of 18 cards and 50+ attack, but Iceman gonna ice

First game i conceded, there was 0 Iceman and 1 Bishop in the first 10ish cards, and already got 2 incursions (and the 3rd one was coming soon, i peaked after conceding). The second game, well, i got to ko almost all greys with bishop and henchmen while recruiting iceman ramps. I got the tactics with extra turn, and then the next turn got just enough attack to hit him 3 times. The other side used Prodigy to copy some recruit and get Fantastic rare, so they focused Kang once too.

Couldn't resist Bring Iceman to the party! SBPKECN built a reasonable Ranged deck, while the snashtried for a Dark Memories deck, but stumbled into Bishop's Rare, which then(through KO power), also got him Iceman's rare as well!!  Game was over shortly after that!!

column33: This was HARD! I guess that's why it was EPIC grim reaper! We managed to pull out a win on the last turn which was pretty epic in itself! Boss was just pretty fat out buffed up with all those locations. Fun but difficult!
She Hulk is underrated

This was so brutally difficult. The first part of the villain deck was all twists and lethal legion. I couldn’t get any traction until the 35th turn! I did manage to get a couple of rares to trigger which was fun.
very fun game trying to manage the city to keep Annihilus' strikes from getting out of control.

Holy cow! We made it to turn 25! My wife hit the MM 2 times in one turn too! I actually thought we might win and then a MS hit with a villain followed by a Twist with another twist filling the city and sending our last escape out of the city. We got so lucky with twists being in the bottom of the deck and hero rares being in the top. All but Wasp's rare were recruited.

This is, without a doubt, the least favorite Legendary setup I've ever played. I ran it 3 times, and lost on turns 6, 14, and 9. Submitted run 2, as if it matters. Chaining twists into master strikes into queens of annihilation into armadas, which bring back henchmen is absolutely the worst ever. Kudos to Kylah for finding the single worst Legendary game ever :)

1st attempt: 4/2 starter hands on both decks and early Twists meant 3 escapes before I even re-shuffled. Then the first Strike hit back-to-back Queens of Annihilation and I was done for. 2nd attempt: Weaponized Galactus was out before I re-shuffled, so I was quickly wounded and down to 4 city spaces. A triple-twist chain finished me off. This is not a set up that I would want to try again in a hurry. I don’t mind the odd hard set up, and I may just be grumpy because I’m recovering from dental surgery, but this is the 2nd in a row that has seemed engineered to be devilishly hard, and it’s not just my pods where I’m seeing comments on the difficulty level. Definitely dampens my enthusiasm for bothering to continue with the Eliminated Pods.

Game 1 we lost turn 6 as the scheme twist and MS just chained together. Game 2 Weaponized Galactus made 3 appearances withing the chain of twist.  
in each game it ended before we could even start using the cards we had recruited really (game 1 we only saw 1 card each). 
I didn't bother to track the villain deck at the end as it's not worth the time so I just picked numbers.
I picked these two to have a KO / bystander combo… ironically the other hand did so much better with Hulkbuster supplemented by a couple key Jocasta and Star-Lord.

Lots of fun with this setup (shame it's the elimination round). I won using a WWH set up, but wasn't too happy with my strategy of taking too long taking care of the helicopters, so I went with GoG set up for next try and wiped out the helicopters very quickly! Much better score as a result.
